title: Astrobotic fires next-generation 'rotating detonation rocket engine' in record-breaking test (video)
summary: Astrobotic successfully tested its next-generation rotating detonation rocket engine prototype in a series of hot-fire tests, marking a milestone for the experimental propulsion technology.

title: AI sped up James Webb Space Telescope data analysis from years to days. What can it do for the groundbreaking Rubin Observatory?
summary: AI has accelerated James Webb Space Telescope data analysis from years to days. It could similarly enhance the Rubin Observatory's ground-based telescope images by sharpening blurry data.

title: NASA wants to use a fleet of MoonFall drones to scout the lunar south pole: 'We believe we can do it'
summary: NASA is planning to deploy a fleet of drones called MoonFall to scout the lunar south pole. The agency believes this ambitious project is feasible.

title: SpaceX launching powerful Falcon Heavy rocket today for 1st time in 18 months: Watch it live
summary: SpaceX is set to launch its Falcon Heavy rocket for the first time in 18 months on April 27, with a live broadcast available. This marks a significant milestone for the company's heavy-lift capabilities.

title: The 'Oscars of Science': Breakthrough Prize 2026 awards over $18 million for discoveries across space, physics and more
summary: The 2026 Breakthrough Prize awarded over $18 million for groundbreaking discoveries in dark matter, quantum physics, gene editing, and nonlinear mathematics. The prestigious 'Oscars of Science' recognized contributions across multiple scientific fields.

title: The moon as you rarely see it: How a photographer captured night and day on the first quarter moon
summary: A photographer captured a unique image of the first quarter moon showing both night and day sides. The achievement was described as incredibly rewarding for accurately representing the moon's appearance.

title: Satellite snaps amazing 36th birthday pic of Hubble Space Telescope (photo)
summary: A private Earth-observing satellite captured a photo of the Hubble Space Telescope on April 23, 2026, one day before its 36th anniversary. The image was taken by the WorldView Legion 4 satellite.

title: I Am Artemis: Peter Rossoni
summary: Peter Rossoni, Orion Artemis II Optical Communications System flight manager, reflects on his childhood inspiration from Apollo missions and his current role in NASA's Artemis II mission, which aims to enable communications for astronauts journeying around the Moon in April 2026.

title: The Day of the Trifid Nebula
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured a detailed image of the Trifid Nebula, 5,000 light-years from Earth, to celebrate the mission's 36th launch anniversary on April 24, 2026.

title: NASA Astronauts to Answer Questions from Missouri Students
summary: NASA astronauts Jessica Meir and Jack Hathaway will answer STEM questions from Missouri students during a live Earth-to-space call from the International Space Station on April 30. The event will be streamed live on NASA's YouTube channel.

title: NASA Celebrates Decade of University Innovation in Aeronautics
summary: NASA's University Leadership Initiative (ULI) has celebrated a decade of fostering aeronautical innovations and workforce development, supporting over 1,100 students across 100 schools.

title: Johnson Leaders Honored by National Space Club & Foundation
summary: Two leaders from NASA's Johnson Space Center were honored by the National Space Club & Foundation for their contributions to human spaceflight. Howard Hu received the Norman L. Baker Astronautics Engineer Award for his technical leadership in multiple human spaceflight efforts.

title: NASA Shares SpaceX Crew-13 Assignments for Space Station Mission
summary: NASA has announced the crew assignments for the SpaceX Crew-13 mission to the International Space Station, including astronauts from NASA and CSA. The mission is scheduled for mid-September and will conduct a long-duration science expedition.

title: NASA Kennedy Prepares Facility for Roman Space Telescope Arrival
summary: NASA's Kennedy Space Center is preparing facilities for the upcoming launch of the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope, scheduled for early September aboard a SpaceX Falcon Heavy rocket from Launch Complex 39A. The telescope aims to deliver unprecedented deep, panoramic views of the cosmos, advancing our understanding of the universe.

title: NASA Welcomes Jordan as 63rd Artemis Accords Signatory
summary: Jordan signed the Artemis Accords, becoming the 63rd signatory nation committed to responsible space exploration. NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman welcomed Jordan during a ceremony at NASA headquarters.

title: Liquid Lifeline: NASA Tech Could Create IV Fluid In Space
summary: NASA is developing technology to produce IV fluid in space, which could be crucial for long-duration crewed missions beyond low Earth orbit. The IV fluid, a mix of sodium chloride and purified water, can treat various medical conditions during spaceflight.

title: SpaceX set to launch Falcon Heavy on Monday after 18 month hiatus
summary: SpaceX is preparing to launch its Falcon Heavy rocket after an 18-month hiatus. This marks a significant return for the heavy-lift vehicle after its last mission.

title: Curiosity Rover uncovers fresh organic clues on Ancient Mars
summary: NASA's Curiosity Rover has discovered new organic compounds on Mars, providing fresh clues about ancient life. The findings mark a significant advancement in the search for extraterrestrial life.

title: Progress MS-34 to launch from Baikonur for ISS cargo delivery
summary: Progress MS-34 is scheduled to launch from Baikonur to deliver cargo to the International Space Station (ISS). This follows the CRS NG-24 mission, which also transported cargo to the ISS two weeks prior.

title: Starship Progress at the Cape with Hoping to Launch this year
summary: SpaceX is making progress on Starship's first Block 3 flight at Cape Canaveral, aiming for a launch this year. The upcoming flight marks a significant milestone for the company's next-generation rocket.

title: NASA and Axiom react to OIG Report on delays in Next-Generation Spacesuit Program
summary: NASA and Axiom Space have responded to a critical report from NASA's Office of Inspector General highlighting delays in the Next-Generation Spacesuit Program. The report raises concerns over the program's timeline and potential impacts on upcoming missions.

title: Launch Preview: GPS, Progress, and Starlink missions to launch during busy week
summary: Seven space missions, including GPS, Progress, and Starlink launches, are scheduled for the week of April 20 using five different launch vehicles across four countries. The busy week highlights diverse activities in the global space industry.

title: AST SpaceMobile’s BlueBird 7 declared lost following New Glenn launch
summary: AST SpaceMobile's BlueBird 7 satellite was declared lost after a failed launch aboard Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket. This marks a setback for the company's satellite broadband ambitions.

title: New Glenn launches on third mission, successfully reuses booster for the first time
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket successfully completed its third mission and reused a booster for the first time. The launch marks a significant milestone in the development of partially reusable heavy-lift vehicles.

title: Vast unveils Astronaut Flight Suit and revolutionary Large Docking Adapter
summary: Vast introduced an astronaut flight suit and a new Large Docking Adapter, showcasing a blend of fashion and functionality for future space missions. The innovations are part of the company's efforts to support commercial space station development.

title: Ship 39 and Booster 19 Both Complete Full Engine Static Fires
summary: Ship 39 and Booster 19 have completed full engine static fire tests as part of the Artemis program. This milestone follows the successful return of Orion, marking progress in NASA's lunar mission preparations.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website is mentioned, but no specific space industry news is provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over its 50-year history. This commemorative publication celebrates the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: Researchers have developed electrostatic capture technology to securely grasp and manipulate objects in space. This innovation could revolutionize satellite servicing and debris removal missions.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully completed space-like tests, remaining on track for a 2027 launch to search for terrestrial planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) invites SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual SME Forum 2026, which includes an online preparatory session in April-May and an in-person event on June 8-9, 2026 at ESA Headquarters in Paris.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: Celeste's first navigation signal was successfully received at an optimal location. The achievement marks a significant milestone for the navigation system's development.

title: First Proba-3 science: surprisingly speedy solar wind
summary: ESA's Proba-3 mission has created 57 artificial solar eclipses since July 2025, collecting over 250 hours of high-resolution solar corona data. Initial findings reveal solar wind structures in the inner corona move 3-4 times faster than previously believed.

title: Register for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026
summary: Registration is now open for ESA's Industry Space Days 2026, an event scheduled for 16–17 September at the agency's technical centre in Noordwijk, The Netherlands.

title: Pointing in the (b)right direction
summary: The article discusses advancements in spacecraft pointing technology. It highlights improvements in precision and efficiency for space missions.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: An international research team demonstrated light-based propulsion using graphene aerogels during ESA's parabolic flight campaign, showing significant thrust in microgravity conditions. The study, led by ULB and Khalifa University, suggests potential applications for steering solar sails and satellite positioning.

title: Meta Secures Overview Energy Space Solar Power Capacity
summary: Meta has secured up to 1 GW of power capacity through Overview Energy's planned solar power-beaming satellite system. This agreement aims to support Meta's energy needs via space-based solar power technology.

title: Pentagon Taps 12 Companies for Golden Dome SBI Tech
summary: The Pentagon has selected 12 companies for the Golden Dome SBI Tech program, awarding contracts worth up to $3.2 billion in total. This initiative aims to enhance space-based defense technologies.

title: Astra Targets Golden Dome With Small Rockets, Says CEO Chris Kemp
summary: Astra's CEO Chris Kemp announced plans to use small rockets for target practice, aiming to improve scalability and reduce costs for commercial and government customers. The initiative is part of Astra's strategy to enhance its launch capabilities.

title: Trump Taps Space Execs For Military Space Roles
summary: Former President Trump has appointed two space industry executives to key military space roles, pending Senate confirmation. The nominees are expected to take up positions in the U.S. Space Force or related defense agencies.

title: Astrobotic Hotfires Engine That Could Power Moon Missions
summary: Astrobotic successfully test-fired a Chakram engine prototype for five minutes at NASA’s Marshall Space Flight Center. The engine could potentially power future Moon missions.

title: Orbital vs. Terrestrial Data Center Cost Analysis
summary: A cost analysis compares the build costs of an orbital data center with a terrestrial one, including opportunity costs for terrestrial delays. The study is presented in a detailed report by Payload Pro.

title: UNIVITY Raises €27M Series A
summary: UNIVITY has raised €27 million in a Series A funding round led by Blast Club and Expansion Venture Capital, with additional support from Bpifrance's Deep Tech fund.

title: Lawmakers Promise to Reject Proposed NASA Cuts—Again
summary: Lawmakers have vowed to reject proposed budget cuts to NASA, stating the proposal cannot support the agency's mandated objectives. The statement was made in response to President Trump's directives for NASA.

title: Tracking Sats From the Sea
summary: Satellites provide domain awareness to protect Navy ships, and some officers suggest the Navy can assist in tracking these satellites. The article discusses potential mutual benefits between satellite tracking and naval operations.

title: ATMOS Space Cargo Raises €25.7M Series A
summary: ATMOS Space Cargo has secured €25.7 million in a Series A funding round, enabling the company to transition to regular operational services. The funds will support the development of ATMOS WORKS and PHOENIX 3, aiming to build a full commercial, institutional, and defense-capable architecture.

title: Space Force Awards $3.2 Billion to 12 Companies for Space-Based Interceptor Work
summary: The U.S. Space Force awarded 12 companies contracts worth up to $3.2 billion to develop space-based interceptor technologies under the Golden Dome initiative. This move aims to enhance missile defense capabilities by leveraging space-based systems.

title: GomSpace to Establish New Ukrainian Joint Venture for Sovereign Comms
summary: GomSpace, a Danish satellite company, is establishing a new joint venture in Ukraine to develop sovereign satellite communications capabilities. The partnership is with Ukrainian firm Stetman to advance this initiative.

title: Rocket Lab Introduces Next-Generation Star Tracker Component
summary: Rocket Lab has launched a next-generation High-Performance Star Tracker (ST-HP) designed for extended spacecraft missions. This new component expands Rocket Lab's offerings in space technology.

title: FCC Rejects Requests for Sharing in MSS Spectrum, Maintaining Exclusive Rights
summary: The FCC rejected requests from SpaceX and other operators to share Mobile Satellite Service (MSS) spectrum, instead maintaining exclusive licensing rights. The decision was formalized in an order issued on Thursday.

title: SDA’s Need for Speed Pushes Startups for Results
summary: The Space Development Agency (SDA) is pausing launches until May to address technology hurdles, supply chain challenges, and balance speed with results. Acting Director Gurpartap 'GP' Sandhoo emphasized the need for startups to deliver results during this strategic pause.

title: SpinLaunch to Use Equinx Data Centers as Teleport Sites for its Constellation
summary: SpinLaunch will collaborate with Equinx to deploy ground infrastructure for its Meridian constellation using 'ultra-compact teleports' at Equinx's data centers. This partnership aims to support SpinLaunch's space launch and satellite operations.

title: Iridium Continues IoT Subscriber Growth in Q1, Desch Talks NTN Direct Launch and Spectrum
summary: Iridium reported a 2% year-over-year revenue increase in Q1 2026, driven by continued growth in IoT subscribers. CEO Matt Desch discussed plans for NTN direct launch and spectrum during the earnings call.

title: Univity Raises Further Funding for VLEO Constellation for Europe
summary: French startup Univity secured a 27 million euro ($32 million) Series A funding round to develop two demonstration satellites for a Very Low Earth Orbit (VLEO) connectivity constellation in Europe.

title: US Officials See Proposed European Union Space Act as Complicated Overreach
summary: U.S. officials criticize the proposed EU Space Act as an overreach, raising concerns about its complexity and potential implications. The draft legislation aims to create a unified regulatory framework for space activities within the European Union.

title: Australian Airline Jetstar Selects Viasat for IFC for Dreamliner Fleet
summary: Jetstar Airways, an Australian low-cost airline, has chosen Viasat's Amara solution to provide in-flight connectivity for its Boeing 787 Dreamliner fleet. The Amara system will be installed on 11 Jetstar aircraft.
